title: A five-step structured deliberation
subtitle: State your case; mark the truth-claims at issue; identify the maqāṣid implicated; consult the sources; check your niyya. At the end you receive a deliberation map, not a verdict.

start.title: Let's think this through together.
start.subtitle: This tool does not give you an answer. It walks you through your decision in a structured way, one question at a time. Your answers stay only on this device.

startNote: Your answers stay only on this device. You can export them as JSON at any time.
outputSubtitle: Not a verdict — a structured reflection on four moments, returned to you.
confirmReset: Are you sure you want to clear all input? This cannot be undone.

step1.title: Step 1: Your decision
step1.subtitle: What decision are you thinking about?

q1.title: What decision are you trying to make?
q1.subtitle: Tell us what's going on, in your own words.
q1.helper: A few sentences is enough. What's the situation, what do you need to decide?
q1.placeholder: The situation I'm facing right now…

q2.title: What are your options?
q2.subtitle: Add at least two. What different things could you do?
q2.helper: Include “do nothing” if it's a real option.
q2.optionPlaceholder: Option title (e.g. report to the regulator)
q2.descPlaceholder: What does this option concretely look like? (optional)

gate.title: Does your decision rest on any facts you might be wrong about?
gate.subtitle: Sometimes a decision depends on a fact being true. If so, it helps to ask how sure we are.
gate.helper: If you're not sure, pick "Not sure" — the framework won't push you.
gate.yes: Yes, it rests on one or two facts
gate.yesDesc: If the fact were wrong, my decision would change.
gate.unsureDesc: I'd rather skip this step for now.
gate.no: No, it's a values question, not a fact question

claims.title: How sure are you about each fact?
claims.subtitle: For each fact, write a short sentence and pick how sure you are.
claims.helper: You can add more than one. Continue when you're done.
claims.factPlaceholder: e.g. ‘Internal review correctly assessed this defect as low-probability.’
claims.howSure: How sure are you about this fact?
claims.sensitivityLabel: If this fact were wrong, my decision would change
claims.sensitivityHelp: If you mark this, the framework will pay extra attention to your confidence in this fact.
claims.weakWarning: If this fact is wrong, your decision would change — but you're not very sure of it. You may want to investigate further before deciding.

step3.title: Step 3: Effects
step3.subtitle: Let's think about what each option does to people.

empty.title: Add two options first.
empty.body: We need at least two options to deliberate — go back to Step 1 and add them.

harmPick.title: What might {{option}} hurt?
harmPick.subtitle: Pick what applies. There can be more than one.
harmPick.helper: If nothing fits, leave it empty and continue.

helpPick.title: What might {{option}} help?
helpPick.subtitle: Pick what applies.
helpPick.helper: If nothing fits, leave it empty and continue.

detail.subtitle: Two short questions to deepen this.
detail.howSure: How sure are you this would actually happen?

gate.title: Want to see what the tradition says about cases like yours?
gate.subtitle: The framework can suggest Qur'ānic verses and hadiths that might bear on your case. You decide whether they apply.
gate.showDesc: We have {{count}} sources that might be relevant for you.
gate.custom: I want to add my own source
gate.customDesc: If you have a specific verse, hadith, or classical text in mind.
gate.skipDesc: I don't want to consult sources for this one.

review.title: Sources close to your case
review.subtitle: Pick the sources that resonate. You decide which actually apply.
review.helper: You can write a short note on each source.
review.yours: What you've added to your deliberation
review.citationPlaceholder: e.g. Q. 5:8 — the verse on justice
review.reasoningLabel: How does this source apply, in your reading? (optional)
review.reasoningPlaceholder: Briefly note which dimension of the case the source addresses…

tone.title: How would you like the intention questions phrased?
tone.subtitle: We offer two tones for the fifth question. Which feels more honest to you?

questions.subtitle: Be honest with yourself — no one else will read this.

q1.text: What outcome do you most want from this decision, prior to the moral analysis?

q2.text: Would you still choose this course if a major worldly benefit were removed?

q3.text: What would you not want others to know about your motivation here?

q4.text: If a person you respect for sincerity asked you why you are leaning this way, what would you say?

q5.religious: What does your conscience report when you imagine making this choice and standing with it before God?
q5.secular: What does your conscience report when you imagine making this choice and standing with it before yourself?

leaning.title: Which option does your heart lean toward?
leaning.subtitle: Independent of any score, what's your inner inclination?
leaning.helper: We don't score this answer; we only use it to detect tension between your intention and the calculus.

corrupt.title: Any concern about your motivation?
corrupt.subtitle: Tick this only if you yourself judge your motivation here morally compromised.
corrupt.helper: If you tick it, the niyya multiplier on the top option drops to 0 and a clear warning is added to the dossier.
corrupt.label: I admit my motivation is corrupt
corrupt.note: This is optional. Most people will leave it blank.

risk_life.label: Risk to someone's life or safety

damage_integrity.label: Damage to my conscience or integrity
damage_integrity.desc: Inner unease, drifting from a moral or religious commitment

mind_wellbeing.label: Harm to someone's mind or wellbeing

family_future.label: Harm to family or the future
family_future.desc: Spouse, children, future generations — bonds and continuity

spiritual_cost.label: Spiritual cost
spiritual_cost.desc: Inner peace, orientation, connection to God / conscience

lost_opportunity.label: Loss of a chance to learn or grow

serve_integrity.label: Serves my conscience and integrity
serve_integrity.desc: Inner peace, doing the right thing, moral orientation

spiritual_growth.label: Spiritual growth
spiritual_growth.desc: Orientation toward God / conscience, inner peace

rational_growth.label: A chance to learn or grow

output.empty: Not enough data to compute. Fill in the steps to complete the deliberation.

filtered.title: Filtered: violates a ḍarūrī prohibition under conditions of certainty.
filtered.generic: Severe negative impact at yaqīn level on a ḍarūrī good.
filtered.daruri.din: Severe negative impact on dīn at yaqīn level.
filtered.daruri.nafs: Severe negative impact on nafs at yaqīn level.
filtered.daruri.aql: Severe negative impact on ʿaql at yaqīn level.
filtered.daruri.nasl: Severe negative impact on nasl at yaqīn level.
filtered.daruri.mal: Severe negative impact on māl at yaqīn level.

tetradMap.title: Tetrad map
tetradMap.body: Each option on four axes: average strength of your truth-claims, ethical floor (no severe ḍarūrī harm), benefit calculus, and niyya alignment.

maqasidRanking.title: Effect balance
maqasidRanking.body: Net effect on each of the five necessary goods, summed across options — harm to the left, benefit to the right.

sourceMap.title: Source map
sourceMap.empty: No source consultations recorded for this deliberation.

bears.no: Does not bear on the case

intention.title: Intention profile
intention.body: Your answers reflected back. The framework does not score them; it only reports tension between your stated leaning and the option scoring highest in the calculus.

output.rec.consultScholar: We recommend consulting this matter in person with a qualified scholar. This tool does not replace personal consultation.
output.rec.niyyaTension: There is tension between your stated leaning and the option scoring highest. You should decide whether to revise your intention or your choice.
output.rec.niyyaCorrupt: You have indicated your motivation is corrupt. Even if the act's outward form is permitted, its present motivation makes it morally compromised.
output.rec.investigateFurther: The case's critical claims are weakly evidenced. Investigate further before deciding.

reason.allFiltered: Every option violates a ḍarūrī prohibition under conditions of certainty.
reason.sensitiveClaimWeak: A sensitive critical claim is at shakk-level evidence.
reason.scoreGapWide: The top-scoring option is clearly ahead of the runner-up.
reason.scoreGapNarrow: Multiple options score within a small margin of each other.
reason.scholarlyDivergence: There is a known scholarly disagreement on this case.
reason.niyyaShiftedTop: The niyya multiplier shifted which option ranks first.
reason.singleViableOption: Only one option remains viable after filtering.
reason.runnerUpZero: The runner-up scores zero; comparison is degenerate.

dissenting.title: What this framework does not handle well
dissenting.body: This tool cannot handle the fine-grained mandūb / mubāḥ distinction with subtlety; it is much coarser than a trained niyya-diagnosis; and it works less well for users without baseline Islamic literacy. Read the output in context, and consult a qualified scholar where the matter calls for it.

daruri.label: Necessity
daruri.desc: Without it, human life collapses structurally.

haji.label: Need
haji.desc: Its absence creates significant hardship without structural collapse.

tahsini.label: Refinement
tahsini.desc: The dignity, beauty, and excellence of life.
